title: Authorizing the use of Emancipation Hall in the Capitol Visitor Center for a ceremony to present the Congressional Gold Medals awarded under the 'Six Triple Eight' Congressional Gold Medal Act of 2021.
plain_summary: This bill gives permission to use a special room called Emancipation Hall in the Capitol Visitor Center for an important ceremony. The ceremony will happen on April 29, 2025, to give out Congressional Gold Medals. These medals were created by a law from 2021 called the Six Triple Eight Congressional Gold Medal Act. The ceremony will honor people who earned these special medals from Congress.
last_action_text: Message on Senate action sent to the House.

sections.title: Section 1  Use of Emancipation Hall for a ceremony to present the Congressional Gold Medals awarded under the Six Triple Eight Congressional Gold Medal Act of two thousand and twenty-one
sections.text: Congress gives permission to use Emancipation Hall in the Capitol Visitor Center on April twenty-ninth, two thousand and twenty-five. This space will be used for a ceremony to give out Congressional Gold Medals. These medals were created under a law called the Six Triple Eight Congressional Gold Medal Act of two thousand and twenty-one. The Architect of the Capitol is the person in charge of the Capitol building. This person can set rules about how to prepare the hall for the ceremony. Any physical setup work for the ceremony must follow the conditions that the Architect of the Capitol decides on.
